subject: Biology
title: What Is Coral?
description: A quick win: understand What Is Coral?.

hook.text: Coral looks like rock, feels like stone, but it's actually millions of tiny animals living together.

buildup.text: Each coral polyp is a soft-bodied animal related to jellyfish, with a ring of stinging tentacles.

discovery.text: Polyps secrete calcium carbonate skeletons. Over centuries, these accumulate into massive reef structures.

twist.text: Coral gets its color from symbiotic algae called zooxanthellae that live inside its tissue.

climax.text: The algae provide up to 90% of the coral's energy through photosynthesis—a perfect partnership.

punchline.text: Not a rock. Not a plant. An animal city.

quiz.question: What are coral polyps?
options: Mineral formations on the sea floor
